channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Everyone wants to eat Nvidia’s lunch
item.description: So far, Nvidia has provided the vast majority of the processors used to train and operate large AI models like the ones that underpin ChatGPT.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: The VA’s size and complexity may be keeping top tech minds away, and veterans pay the price
item.description: "You begin with the end in mind and understanding the success metrics that are going to be important to drive this," said Sean O'Connor The post The VA’s size and complexity may be keeping top tech minds away, and veterans pay the price first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gerton, Federal News Network

item.title: Cloudflare says it’s investigating the outage that brought down Zoom and LinkedIn
item.description: Internet infrastructure company Cloudflare on Friday said it was investigating an outage that took place in the morning that brought down several global websites including LinkedIn, Zoom and others, the second such crash to affect the company in less than three weeks.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Massachusetts court hears arguments in lawsuit alleging Meta designed apps to be addictive to kids
item.description: Massachusetts’ highest court heard oral arguments Friday in the state’s lawsuit arguing that Meta designed features on Facebook and Instagram to make them addictive to young users.
item.dc:creator: Michael Casey, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: US security agency urges Android and iPhone users to stop using personal VPNs
item.description: CISA warned that many commercial VPNs could be putting your data at greater risk. But that doesn't mean you should give up on privacy – here's what you need to know.
item.dc:creator: Rene Millman, TechRadar

item.title: Chat Control: EU Commissioner backs Parliament line on targeted monitoring
item.description: As the trialogue negotiations are set to begin on Tuesday, December 9, the debate on the controversial Child Sexual Abuse Regulation (CSAR) flares up.
item.dc:creator: Chiara Castro, TechRadar

item.title: Silicon Valley meets the court: Tech trio powering padel’s rise in Bay Area
item.description: Park Padel showcases its flagship South San Francisco club, expanding the sport’s reach across the Bay Area
item.dc:creator: Ryan Macasero, The Mercury News

item.title: Samsung’s Ballie robot is delayed again – and now we know why
item.description: With Ballie now past its planned 2025 debut, Samsung tells TechRadar it is “continuing to refine and perfect the technology” before release.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Krol, TechRadar

item.title: Cloudflare says service restored after outage that brought down sites including Zoom and LinkedIn
item.description: Cloudflare reports that it has resolved an outage that disrupted several global websites, including LinkedIn and Zoom.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Digital twin tech is a double-edged sword
item.description: A digital twin is a simulation of an object, system, process, or person. The ability to make and use digital twins is one of the many new opportunities for business that didn’t exist for most businesses before the recent LLM-based AI revolution.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Nvidia boss Jensen Huang steers Trump, Congress against AI chip limits and state-level AI rules
item.description: Nvidia’s Jensen Huang successfully lobbied Trump and lawmakers against a proposed AI chip export rule and issued a warning about fragmented state regulation of AI.
item.dc:creator: Eric Hal Schwartz, TechRadar

item.title: OpenAI must adapt to a world where it’s no longer invincible
item.description: Welcome to AI Decoded, Fast Company’s weekly newsletter that breaks down the most important news in the world of AI. I’m Mark Sullivan, a senior writer at Fast Company, covering emerging tech, AI, and tech policy.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Jeff Bezos calls his AI company ‘Project Prometheus.’ So does this California lawyer
item.description: Jeff Bezos last month went public with his new AI firm, which is currently being called Project Prometheus. The effort had been in development for a while, but is still relatively secretive. There’s no website and only a sparse LinkedIn page describing itself as “AI for the physical economy.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Heilweil, Fast Company

item.title: US federal software reform bill aims to strengthen software management controls
item.description: Software management struggles that have pained enterprises for decades cause the same anguish to government agencies, and a bill making its way through the US House of Representatives to strengthen controls around government software management holds lessons for enterprises too.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Tesla reliability improves sharply in new Consumer Reports study
item.description: Tesla Inc. made its first appearance in the top 10 for reliability in Consumer Reports' rankings released Thursday, a stark turnaround for the electric vehicle maker that was a laggard just a few years ago.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: A proactive defense against npm supply chain attacks
item.description: Open-source software has become the backbone of modern development, but with that dependency comes a widening attack surface. The npm ecosystem in particular has been a high-value target for adversaries who know that one compromised package can cascade downstream into thousands of applications.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Seven coding domains no developer really understands
item.description: We all want to be thought competent by our peers—to have them think we know what we are doing. And for the most part, we do, right? But come on, let’s be honest. There are a few things that just make our heads spin.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Four things AWS needs to fix at re:Invent this week
item.description: The mood among Amazon Web Services customers is shifting from curiosity to urgency as the company prepares to once again to “re:Invent” itself at its annual customer conference this week.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Real Apple support emails used in new phishing scam
item.description: Scammers nearly stole an Apple account by exploiting the support system with authentic-looking tickets and phone calls, users can protect themselves with safety steps.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Check if your passwords were stolen in huge leak
item.description: Threat intelligence firm Synthient uncovers one of the largest password exposures ever, prompting immediate security recommendations.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
